pr_description:
It seems like R.equals does a lot of work even if passed the same reference, for example:

```js
const x = {/* big structure */}
R.equals(x, x) // does deep compare
```

Is there a reason that it doesn't have an `Object.is` short-circuit?
commit_message: perf: faster equals with Object.is short circuit
diff_text:
diff --git a/source/equals.spec.js b/source/equals.spec.js
index 6d238eb..2e0824a 100644
--- a/source/equals.spec.js
+++ b/source/equals.spec.js
@@ -309,7 +309,7 @@ describe('brute force', () => {
 {
   "ERRORS_MESSAGE_MISMATCH": 0,
   "ERRORS_TYPE_MISMATCH": 0,
-  "RESULTS_MISMATCH": 8,
+  "RESULTS_MISMATCH": 0,
   "SHOULD_NOT_THROW": 0,
   "SHOULD_THROW": 0,
   "TOTAL_TESTS": 289,
